creator: destination_wellknown
caption: This British spa hotel was very close to getting a top rating because of its great food & fantastic young staff, but the last few hours of my stay ended up wrecking the experience a bit 😩  📍Doxford Hall Hotel & Spa   I had a package deal that included a 3-course dinner and breakfast as well as a night in a standard room which cost £190.   That’s pretty good value for money, in my opinion, especially considering the food (the evening meal, at least) was excellent 🙌  So I was thrilled when I went down for breakfast and thought I’d found a proper hidden gem here 😎   Breakfast was supposed to be from 07, but when I went there at around 7:20 I had to ask a member of staff if they needed my room number or something (because I was ignored) 😅   The waitress said they weren’t quite ready and it’d be around 10 more minutes. No bother, I went for a wee walk in the beautiful gardens and saw a pheasant! 😀  I returned, sat down, was given the food menu I’m showing in this video, and ordered some coffee and toast. The waitress returned with my coffee and toast after a few minutes - and then she just left straight away 😅   So after a couple of minutes of me trying to make eye contact with her, I went up and put in my order for an avocado toast to her colleague (who were very nice).  My original waitress then came out and told me, in a rather rude tone, that she was coming to get my order 😤  An uncomfortable situation to be put in as a customer, and it could have been sorted out so easily with the proper communication from my waitress. Let me know how “it works,” and then keep and then make sure actually to take my order 🤓  What do you think about the hotel - would you stay here? 👇  #hotelreview #travelguide #travelblogger #england #britain #lovegreatbritain #hotels

transcription.text: My stay at this spa hotel in England started so well but ended on a sour note. So I drove from the Holy Island to Doxford Hall and Spa where I was pretty surprised by how nice the outdoor bit of the hotel was. The inside was a bit more run down but I think that's kind of to be expected and maybe part of the charm when you're at a hotel that was built over 200 years ago. The spa area consisted of a pool, a sauna and a steam room so that was a bit of a letdown. But the dinner was incredible and the staff were all very young adults who were so nice and friendly and the food was some of the best that I got on this trip through the north east of England. So I was very excited when I went down for breakfast the next day but the mood had changed and the new waitress seemed a bit annoyed with me and my toast was burned. So unfortunately I can only give this hotel 4 out of 6 stars.
